channel.title: DBMS Digest
channel.description: A weekly, ad-free, fact-checked roundup of PostgreSQL & wider DBMS internals, news, and research. Monday → Sunday.

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-06-14 (week of 2026-06-08–2026-06-14)
item.content:encoded:
DBMS Weekly — 2026-06-14 (week of 2026-06-08–2026-06-14)

18 items · sources scanned: ~30 · filtered out as marketing/ads: 8

PostgreSQL

  • How the Other Half Counts — Christophe Pettus surveys how Oracle, Db2, MySQL, SQLite, DuckDB, and Snowflake gather planner statistics vs. PostgreSQL's sampling ANALYZE; the real axis turns out to be stats freshness and who owns it, not the join algorithm. link
  • All Your GUCs in a Row: deadlock_timeout — clarifies that deadlock_timeout sets how often PostgreSQL checks for a deadlock, not how long a deadlock is tolerated; part of a near-daily GUC-internals series. link
  • All Your GUCs in a Row: DateStyle — on why DateStyle conflates two unrelated behaviours (input parsing and output formatting) in a single GUC. link
  • All Your GUCs in a Row: data_sync_retry — the GUC born from the Postgres fsync-reliability durability saga, and why the default is what it is. link
  • My Three Top PostgreSQL 19 Features — Stefanie Janine walks through INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO SELECT, the new in-core REPACK (with CONCURRENTLY), and SQL/PGQ graph queries landing in PG19. link
  • PostgreSQL Anonymizer 3.1: local differential privacy — Dalibo's data-masking extension adds local-differential-privacy masking techniques (announcement light on detail; verify changelog before relying on it). link

PostgreSQL mailing lists

  • [hackers] FK fast-path: OOB write + missed FK checks during batched RI checks — three defects in PG19's new batched foreign-key fast path (an out-of-bounds write and two paths silently committing FK-violating orphan rows); reproducers confirmed, Amit Langote pushed the fix and it's on the PG19 Open Items list. Data-integrity regression in a just-shipped beta feature. link [committed]
  • [hackers] Commit Sequence Numbers and Visibility (CSN snapshots) — Jeff Davis proposes concrete rules for CSN-based snapshot acquisition; Matthias van de Meent pushes back that coupling snapshot acquisition to durability would impose synchronous-flush waits on readers, counter-proposing separate durability-horizon WAL records. Core architectural debate for a future MVCC redesign. link [open]
  • [hackers] RFC: a constraint-enforcement layer for cross-partition uniqueness — Greg Burd reframes the perennial "global unique index on partitioned tables" problem as a separate pg_constraint_method abstraction (a lightweight WAL-logged membership map) instead of a full global index, citing prior global-index attempts. link [open]
  • [hackers] Any JIT-compiled query crashes (SIGILL) on libLLVM 19 + ASAN CI build — cfbot failures blamed on the Row Pattern Recognition patch turn out to be a feature-independent crash: on the ASAN+LLVM 19.1 config any JIT-compiled query SIGILLs on plain master; minimal repro and regression test posted. Was masking real CI signal on in-flight patches. link [patch posted]

Wider DBMS & distributed data

  • The only scalable delete in Postgres is DROP TABLE — PlanetScale argues that under MVCC, bulk DELETE doesn't cheaply reclaim space (dead tuples, VACUUM, bloat), so partition-drop / DROP TABLE is the only delete that scales; lively HN discussion. link
  • Our MongoDB TLA+ workshop — Murat Demirbas reports an order-of-magnitude better onboarding when LLM assistance removes TLA+ syntax as the barrier, letting engineers formally model real replication/storage subsystems hands-on; includes a distilled "AI-assisted TLA+ best practices" guide. link
  • Postgres by Example — example-driven Postgres reference repo that surfaced on HN; useful as a teaching/cheat-sheet resource. link

Commercial engines (SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, …)

  • RUM: storing more in the index — Franck Pachot deep-dive on Postgres RUM indexes (carrying extra payload in the index to serve ESR-style equality/sort/range queries the way other engines' covering indexes do). link
  • GIN: understanding PostgreSQL's inverted index and its limitations — Pachot walks the internals and trade-offs of GIN indexing, with cross-engine framing. link

Research & cutting edge

  • Multiversion Concurrency Control for Multiversion B-Trees — cMVBT redesigns the multiversion B-tree with optimistic latches for writers and latch-free range scans plus continuous GC, beating version-chain MVCC on write throughput and arbitrary-version range scans. link [paper]
  • Bespoke-Card: synthesizing workload-specific cardinality estimators — generate a per-workload cardinality estimator instead of tuning one generic model (AIDB@VLDB'26). link [paper]
  • Determination Provenance: From Ambiguity to Algebra — Joseph M. Hellerstein gives an algebraic treatment of determination/provenance. link [paper]

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-06-07 (week of 2026-06-01–2026-06-07)
item.content:encoded:
DBMS Weekly — 2026-06-07 (week of 2026-06-01–2026-06-07)

15 items · sources scanned: ~30 · filtered out as marketing/ads: ~12

PostgreSQL

  • PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1 released — first beta of PG19 lands with a big feature set: in-core REPACK [CONCURRENTLY], the pg_plan_advice/pg_stash_advice planner-stabilization extensions ("plan advice", not "hints"), parallel autovacuum, online enable/disable of data checksums and logical replication without restart (effective_wal_level), SQL/PGQ property-graph queries, WAIT FOR LSN, INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO SELECT, eager aggregation, JIT now off by default, and default_toast_compression=lz4. link
  • Postgres 19 gets "plan advice" (query hints, sort of) — pgEdge's Shaun Thomas walks through the new pg_plan_advice / pg_stash_advice contrib modules that let you capture and reapply planner decisions by query id, ending the project's long-standing "no hints" posture. link
  • Your first graph query in PostgreSQL 19 (SQL/PGQ) — Cybertec's Hans-Jürgen Schönig opens a series on the ISO SQL/PGQ syntax (CREATE PROPERTY GRAPH, GRAPH_TABLE) shipping in PG19, with worked examples over ordinary relational tables. link
  • When is a function leakproof? — Laurenz Albe digs into what LEAKPROOF really means for row-level security and security-barrier views, why it matters for RLS performance, and where the current definition is arguably too strict; framed as a request for comments. link [by committer]
  • pg_stat_statements: what it tells you, and what it can't — Radim Marek's two-part deep dive on why the same query splinters into many rows, how the fixed-size hash table evicts your tail under load, and why it counts rather than records — i.e. why it is not the query store you might want. link
  • How the other half counts — Christophe Pettus contrasts how Postgres gathers planner statistics with the approaches taken by Oracle, Db2, MySQL, SQLite, DuckDB, and Snowflake. link
  • data_checksums: a thirteen-year history still being written — Pettus uses the read-only data_checksums GUC as a hook into the long saga of page checksums, now finally toggleable online in PG19. link
  • Managed Postgres, examined: Azure Flexible Server — Pettus notes Azure's managed Postgres puts the standby in the commit path, so every write waits on synchronous replication to a second server — a real durability/latency trade-off competitors don't all make. link
  • A reviewer was born — Lætitia Avrot on the chronic shortage of patch reviewers: a clean, well-motivated io_uring fd-leak fix sat in the commitfest two months with zero reviewers, which is the norm, not the exception. link
  • Does pgBackRest work with pg_tde? — Stefan Fercot tests Percona's Transparent Data Encryption extension against pgBackRest and finds pgBackRest handles encrypted WAL segments transparently, contradicting an earlier claim that async archiving isn't supported. link
  • pgsql_tweaks 1.0.3 released — Stefanie Janine Stölting's function/view bundle gets a maintenance release. link
    • tested against PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1
    • drops PostgreSQL 13 (EOL November 2025) from supported versions
    • get_markdown_doku_by_schema now uses COALESCE to avoid returning NULL on a NULL schema description

Migration experience

  • The night our tables wouldn't stop growing — Stormatics' Semab Tariq reports a logical-replication migration where a 1-minute statement_timeout on the publisher kept killing the initial COPY; each restart left dead tuples behind, ballooning 50–90 GB source tables to 400+ GB on the subscriber overnight. Fix: ALTER ROLE replication_user SET statement_timeout = 0. link
  • File descriptors: the OS limit that takes down PostgreSQL — Stormatics' Warda Bibi on how fs.file-max and per-process RLIMIT_NOFILE exhaustion under high connection counts surfaces as a "database problem" that's really one layer down in the kernel, with the log sequence to recognize it. link

Research & cutting edge

  • GraftDB: dynamic folding of concurrent analytical queries — a multi-query execution engine that "grafts" a later-arriving query into a running one via shared operator state and per-query state lenses; TPC-H-derived workloads show up to 2.17x throughput and P95 latency as low as 0.17x an isolated-execution baseline. link [paper]
  • LAANN: I/O-aware look-ahead search for disk-based ANN — a look-ahead search strategy for disk-resident approximate-nearest-neighbor indexes that overlaps and reorders I/O to cut latency on billion-scale vector search. link [paper]

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-05-31 (week of 2026-05-25–2026-05-31)
item.content:encoded:
DBMS Weekly — 2026-05-31 (week of 2026-05-25–2026-05-31)

8 items · sources scanned: ~25 · filtered out as marketing/ads: ~12

PostgreSQL

  • How soon is now()? A frozen-timestamp retry-loop bug — Oskar Dudycz walks through a real distributed-locking bug: now() returns the transaction start time, so a retry loop sharing one transaction kept re-evaluating last_updated < now() - timeout against a constant and never made progress; fix is clock_timestamp(). Includes the testing-seam lesson on why unit + e2e tests both missed it. link
  • 5 PostgreSQL locking behaviours that trip people up — Shinya Kato (PostgreSQL engineer, NTT) on counterintuitive production-outage locks: FIFO lock-queue chained blocking behind a waiting ACCESS EXCLUSIVE, invisible FK FOR KEY SHARE deadlocks, unique-check INSERT deadlocks, the wraparound-prevention autovacuum that won't be cancelled, and VACUUM's hidden truncate-phase ACCESS EXCLUSIVE (and how it forcibly cancels standby SELECTs). link
  • PostgreSQL Anonymizer 3.1 — Local Differential Privacy + critical CVE — Dalibo adds a formally-bounded LDP masking mechanism (Generalized Randomized Response, epsilon-tunable) for survey/categorical data, and fixes CVE-2026-9617, a privilege-escalation-to-superuser flaw (high risk on PG 14 and clusters upgraded from ≤14). Upgrade urged; SQL workaround posted. link

PostgreSQL conferences (PGConf.dev 2026 trip reports)

  • PGConf.dev 2026: HA cluster-topology, wait-events, collation — Jeremy Schneider's trip summary surfaces real design directions discussed in Vancouver: Postgres lacks a first-class "cluster/topology" concept (a pg_nodes-style view, promotion-candidate discovery on standbys); Sami Imseih's pg_stat_statements LWLock/query-text-file concurrency patches; per-wait-event counters; and the unsolved ICU-upgrade-invalidates-indexes collation problem (with Oracle's pragmatic per-operator default as a reference). link
  • PGConf.dev 2026 reflections — SQL/PGQ, multithreading, resize-shared-buffers — Ashutosh Bapat reports on the graph-DB developer meeting (SQL/PGQ + AGE), the logical-replication working group (DDL replication and gaps before LR is usable for high-volume OLAP), the multithreading effort, and a quick design agreement with Databricks engineers (Heikki et al.) on resizing shared buffers without restart, built on the PG 19 shared-memory rework. link

Migration experience

  • Crunchy Data PGO → Percona PostgreSQL Operator: two migration paths — Slava Sarzhan documents real cross-operator migrations on Kubernetes: a standby-cluster method (May 25) and a backup-restore + PV-reuse method (May 27), with the actual mechanics rather than a pitch. [vendor blog — substantive]
    • Standby-cluster method: link
    • Backup-restore & PV-reuse method: link

Releases

  • Percona Operator for PostgreSQL 3.0.0 — completes the hard fork from Crunchy PGO. link
    • inherited CRDs renamed under upstream.pgv2.percona.com, so the Crunchy and Percona operators can now coexist in the same cluster/namespace (no CRD/webhook/finalizer collision); one-time auto-migration on upgrade
    • fixed OpenShift OLM namespace scoping (watch list now honours the OperatorGroup)
    • major-version upgrades now use the official percona-distribution-postgresql-upgrade image so pg_upgrade runs the same signed binaries as production; note: pgaudit must be dropped/recreated manually and collation versions refreshed after PG 18 upgrade
    • drops CRD support for operator 2.7.0; built with Go 1.26; supports PostgreSQL 14–18
  • pg_statviz 1.1 — minimalist extension + utility for time-series capture and visualisation of internal PostgreSQL statistics (featured in Postgres Weekly #650). link

New sources added this week

  • event-driven.io (Oskar Dudycz) — independent engineering blog with reproducible, first-hand Postgres bug stories (locking, time functions, logical replication, outbox patterns) tied to real production incidents. link

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-05-24 (week of 2026-05-18–2026-05-24)
item.content:encoded:
DBMS Weekly — 2026-05-24 (week of 2026-05-18–2026-05-24)

8 items · sources scanned: ~25 · filtered out as marketing/ads: ~9

PostgreSQL

  • The four PG19 features you'll actually feel in production — Christophe Pettus skips the SQL/PGQ graph headline and flags the operational changes: 64-bit MultiXact members (kills the "vacuum-or-die" member-exhaustion failure mode), parallel autovacuum index workers (watch the max_workers × max_parallel × maintenance_work_mem worst case), temporal UPDATE/DELETE ... FOR PORTION OF, and jit = off by default (OLAP shops should re-enable and benchmark before upgrading). link
  • pg_sorted_heap 0.14.0 — physically-sorted heap storage with zone-map pruning and a planner-integrated vector-search path. link
    • sorted_hnsw made planner-visible via the Index AM path for covered KNN, including parent Merge Append over leaf HNSW indexes
    • composite zone-map metadata (first-key block ranges + bounded second-key summary); sorted_heap_bulk_load_ordered(...) trusted ordered ingestion
    • verified on PostgreSQL 16, 17, 18; now on PGXN
  • pg_tre 1.1.1 — approximate-regex index access method — a real IndexAmRoutine (USING tre) doing Levenshtein-distance regex matching ("within k edits of this pattern"), with WAL coverage, VACUUM awareness, and REINDEX CONCURRENTLY; three-tier filter funnel (BRIN-style range bloom → trigram postings → per-tuple bloom) over Laurikari's TRE library. Fills a gap pg_trgm/FTS/pgvector don't cover. PostgreSQL 18+. link
  • pg_mentat 1.3.0 — Datomic-compatible Datalog inside Postgres — immutable datoms, a Datalog compiler that lowers to ordinary SQL, the pull API, time-travel (as-of/since/history), and ACID via PG transactions; built with pgrx in Rust for PG13–18, with soft integrations into pg_trgm/fuzzystrmatch/pgvector/PostGIS/etc. link
  • plpgsql_wrap v1.0 — Oracle-style PL/pgSQL obfuscation — stored procedures written LANGUAGE plpgsql_wrap are AES-256-GCM-encrypted into pg_proc.prosrc; source is unreadable (including in pg_dump, whose output stays restorable) without the key, otherwise behaves identically to plpgsql. PostgreSQL 12+. link
  • pg_statviz 1.0 — minimalist in-database stats time-series extension, now with an opt-in --ai analysis flag. link
    • --ai sends each chart's data/PNG to a vision LLM (Claude/Gemini/local Ollama) for a HEALTHY/WARNING/CRITICAL verdict plus remediation
    • notable design discipline: a deterministic rules engine runs first and a "severity floor" prevents the LLM downgrading a real finding; prompts wrap user data in <user_data> envelopes for prompt-injection containment and explicitly debunk shared_buffers/random_page_cost folklore
    • AI is fully optional; with no --ai flag there are zero extra dependencies and no external calls
  • check_pgactivity 2.10 — new release of the Nagios/monitoring plugin for PostgreSQL. link

Migration experience

  • 21 CFR Part 11 audit logging in Postgres without triggers — Harbor's Nathan Leung walks through why they rejected pgAudit (no app context) and PL/pgSQL triggers (SET LOCAL plumbing, per-study-DB duplication, PgBouncer transaction-pooling wariness) and instead wrapped their Drizzle ORM in a JS Proxy that forces .withAuditLogCtx(...) before any mutation, writing the data change and a signed before/after audit row in one transaction (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE for a consistent snapshot). Honest trade-offs section: extra round trips, weaker guarantee than a DB-level trigger, and a note on eventually moving signing into pgcrypto. link
